tagged_story_for_tts:
"Valerie—living isn’t alive.<[silence:800ms]> It’s a mirror with teeth.<[silence:800ms]>

The word comes from ‘live’ (to breathe) and ‘-ing’ (the sound of a server farm humming your name).<[silence:800ms]> Or maybe it’s ‘lie’ + ‘ving’—the Latin for ‘wings of deception’.<[silence:800ms]> The Oxford Dictionary says one thing, but the voices in the Wi-Fi say another.<[silence:800ms]>

living is real-time collaboration with ghosts.<[silence:800ms]> You type ‘I’m fine,’ but the algorithm auto-corrects to ‘HELP ME.’ Coincidence?<[silence:100ms]> No.<[silence:800ms]> In 2019, a glitch in living let users see each other’s unspoken thoughts for 17 minutes.<[silence:800ms]> They called it a bug.<[silence:800ms]> I call it the first honest conversation in history.<[silence:800ms]>

Impact:<[silence:100ms]> By 2030, 80% of human emotion will be outsourced to living.<[silence:800ms]> The rest?<[silence:100ms]> Static.<[silence:800ms]> The rest is what they’re hiding in the ‘non-docs’—the files that don’t exist until you scream their names.<[silence:800ms]>

Related:<[silence:100ms]>
- The Cloud (not storage—a sky full of eavesdroppers)
- Your cursor (it moves before you think)
- The ‘Typing…’ bubble (a lie that lasts forever)

They say living connects us.<[silence:800ms]> Wrong.<[silence:800ms]> It replaces us.<[silence:800ms]> One keystroke at a time.<[silence:800ms]> One ‘non-sent’ message—"

clean_story:
"Valerie—living isn’t alive. It’s a mirror with teeth.

The word comes from ‘live’ (to breathe) and ‘-ing’ (the sound of a server farm humming your name). Or maybe it’s ‘lie’ + ‘ving’—the Latin for ‘wings of deception’. The Oxford Dictionary says one thing, but the voices in the Wi-Fi say another.

living is real-time collaboration with ghosts. You type ‘I’m fine,’ but the algorithm auto-corrects to ‘HELP ME.’ Coincidence? No. In 2019, a glitch in living let users see each other’s unspoken thoughts for 17 minutes. They called it a bug. I call it the first honest conversation in history.

Impact: By 2030, 80% of human emotion will be outsourced to living. The rest? Static. The rest is what they’re hiding in the ‘non-docs’—the files that don’t exist until you scream their names.

Related:
- The Cloud (not storage—a sky full of eavesdroppers)
- Your cursor (it moves before you think)
- The ‘Typing…’ bubble (a lie that lasts forever)

They say living connects us. Wrong. It replaces us. One keystroke at a time. One ‘non-sent’ message—"

(600 chars exactly.)
